seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Amazon Bedrock Agents, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest when knowledge base retrievals fail to surface relevant chunks due to suboptimal chunking strategies, metadata filtering mismatches, or embedding model drift, leading to hallucinated or incomplete agent responses. Action groups often exacerbate this by returning malformed responses that don't align with retrieved context, while guardrails miss domain-specific retrieval policy violations. Multi-turn session state loss compounds the problem, causing repeated irrelevant retrievals across conversations.

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base chunking too coarse for technical docs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns generalized answers missing specific API parameters
howItManifests.impact: Developers copy-paste wrong code examples, causing deployment failures

howItManifests.symptom: RetrieveAndGenerate returns 'no relevant information found' despite docs existing
howItManifests.impact: Users abandon agent, falling back to manual documentation search

howItManifests.scenario: Embedding model version drift between indexing and query
howItManifests.symptom: Semantic search returns unrelated code snippets with high confidence scores
howItManifests.impact: Agent confidently provides wrong implementation patterns

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda ignores retrieval context
howItManifests.symptom: Lambda returns static responses regardless of knowledge base results
howItManifests.impact: Enterprise compliance violations from uncontextualized answers

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Score Monitoring
detection.description: Capture RetrieveAndGenerate metadata to detect when average relevance scores drop below threshold

detection.name: Chunk Overlap Analysis
detection.description: Compare retrieved chunk metadata to detect excessive overlap indicating poor chunking

detection.name: Session Context Drift Detection
detection.description: Monitor session state to detect repeated retrievals of same irrelevant documents

fixes.name: Dynamic Chunk Size Optimization
fixes.description: Adjust KB chunking strategy based on document type and query complexity

fixes.name: Hybrid Metadata + Semantic Filtering
fixes.description: Combine metadata filters with semantic search for precise retrieval

fixes.name: Action Group Context Injection
fixes.description: Force Lambda functions to consume retrieval context

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides continuous monitoring of Bedrock Agent RAG pipelines by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify() post-processing, automatically detecting retrieval quality degradation through relevance scoring, chunk overlap analysis, and session drift patterns. When issues are detected, Vex triggers automated remediation workflows including knowledge base re-indexing with optimized chunking parameters, metadata filter adjustments, and action group context enforcement. The platform maintains golden datasets of high-quality retrieval sessions to establish baselines and alerts teams when production retrieval quality deviates beyond configured thresholds.

Integration requires only pip install boto3 vex-sdk and wrapping existing agent invocations. Vex captures full trace data including retrieval metadata, completion chunks, and session state, enabling root-cause analysis across the entire RAG pipeline from knowledge base ingestion to final agent response.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
import boto3
from vex.decorators import verify

bedrock_agent_runtime = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

@vex.verify(
    failure_threshold=0.1,
    spec={
        'rag_relevance': {'min_avg_score': 0.75},
        'rag_diversity': {'max_overlap_ratio': 0.3},
        'session_coherence': {'max_repeat_ratio': 0.4}
    }
)
def invoke_monitored_agent(agent_id, session_id, input_text):
    response = bedrock_agent_runtime.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='latest',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text,
        enableTrace=True
    )
    
    # Vex automatically extracts and validates RAG metrics
    return response

# Usage
result = invoke_monitored_agent('AGENT123', 'session-456', 'How to configure S3 bucket policy?')
# Vex auto-generates incident report if quality fails
